options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of items to return

list.usage_instructions: Use `keap items list` when you need to list items. Key options: --table/-t, --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p.

get.help: Get details for a specific item.
get.usage_instructions: Use `keap items get` when you need to get details for a specific item. Key options: --table/-t, --properties/-p.

search.help: Search items with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields.
search.usage_instructions: Use `keap items search` when you need to search items with wildcard pattern matching. supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields. Key options: --table/-t, --limit/-l, --fields, --properties/-p.

items.usage_instructions: Use `keap items` for manage keap items. Consult the leaf commands in this group for the exact action.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth login` when you need to configure authentication credentials. prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. for oauth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent. Key options: --profile/-p, --force/-F, --credential-type/-c.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth logout` when you need to clear stored credentials. Key options: --profile/-p.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth status` when you need to check authentication status across profiles. performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``. Key options: --profile/-p, --table/-t.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth test` when you need to test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles. Key options: --table/-t, --verbose/-v, --profile/-p.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth profiles list` when you need to list all profiles and show which is the default. Key options: --table/-t, --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth profiles get` when you need to get details for a specific profile. Key options: --table/-t.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth profiles create` when you need to create a new profile from .env.example template.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth profiles set-default` when you need to set a profile as the default (is_default_profile=1).

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth profiles delete` when you need to delete a profile and its data. Key options: --force/-F.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth profiles` for manage authentication profiles. Consult the leaf commands in this group for the exact action.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `keap auth` for manage keap authentication. Consult the leaf commands in this group for the exact action.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use `keap cache clear` when you need to remove all cached responses. deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `keap cache` for manage response cache. Consult the leaf commands in this group for the exact action.

usage_instructions: Use `keap` when you need to work with cli interface for keap api. Check the command groups below for the exact operation and required flags.
